title: NAV: Calculate the Net Acquisition Value (NAV) if Company A is...
summary: Calculate the net acquisition value by combining firm values and subtracting premiums and expenses.
prompt: Calculate the Net Acquisition Value (NAV) if Company A is valued at $300 million, Company B at $200 million, with a premium of $50 million and acquisition expenses of $30 million.
solution: NAV = (Value A + Value B) - Premium - Expenses = ($300 million + $200 million) - $50 million - $30 million = $120 million.
